faqs.question: How do I know if I have a clothes moth infestation in my Queens Village home?
faqs.answer: Signs of clothes moth infestation include small holes in wool clothing or carpets, silky webbing in fabric folds, small cream-colored larvae in closets or drawers, and adult moths flying near wardrobes. You may also notice cocoons attached to fabric or in room corners. If you spot any of these signs in your Queens Village home, contact us immediately for an inspection.

faqs.question: What does clothes moth control service cost in Queens Village?
faqs.answer: Clothes moth control in Queens Village typically ranges from $550 to $5,000, depending on the severity of the infestation, the size of your property, and the number of treatment areas required. Larger homes or severe infestations requiring multiple treatments and preventative services will be at the higher end. We provide free inspections and detailed quotes before beginning any work.

faqs.question: How long does clothes moth treatment take to work in Queens Village homes?
faqs.answer: Initial treatment results are often visible within 24-48 hours as adult moths are eliminated, but complete eradication typically requires 2-4 weeks to address all life stages including eggs and larvae. Most Queens Village properties require follow-up treatments scheduled 2-3 weeks apart to ensure all emerging moths are eliminated. We'll provide a customized treatment schedule based on your specific infestation.

faqs.question: Can clothes moths damage more than just clothing in Queens Village apartments?
faqs.answer: Yes, clothes moths can damage any natural fiber materials including wool carpets, upholstered furniture, curtains, blankets, and even wall hangings or tapestries. In Queens Village's older homes and apartments, they commonly infest stored items in attics, vintage clothing collections, and wool area rugs. Our comprehensive treatment protects all vulnerable fabrics throughout your property.
